LHX.sentence: L3Harris ticked up slightly, but it's badly lagged the other big defense contractors over the past month, which sets it up as the one that could catch back up — nothing's wrong with the underlying business.

ASML.sentence: ASML slipped a touch, but the story hasn't changed — they're the sole maker of the most advanced chipmaking machines, and demand is expected to grow as AI drives a new wave of chip production.

LIN.sentence: Linde is having a quiet stretch — industrial gas demand is steady, they keep nudging prices up under long-term contracts, and it's priced right in line with its peers.

MSFT.sentence: Microsoft is way down from its peak even though the Azure cloud and AI story keeps chugging along — the price has pulled back a lot more than the business has.

TMO.sentence: Thermo Fisher slipped a little, but biotech funding is thawing, and since they supply the equipment every lab needs, more money flowing into research eventually flows to them.

ENB.sentence: Enbridge is steady as its pipelines keep collecting tolls, though the dividend isn't paying much more than safe government bonds right now, so it's a touch expensive.

NOW.sentence: ServiceNow bounced nicely today, but it's fallen dramatically from its high — companies still love its workflow-automation and AI tools, so the big drop looks more like the crowd rethinking the price than the business breaking.

GLNCY.sentence: Glencore was basically flat — the mining and commodity-trading giant is being fairly valued for both its copper mines and its trading desk, with nothing dramatic happening.

IBIT.sentence: Bitcoin drifted down slightly, and worth noting that buying these dips hasn't paid off well this cycle, so patience is warranted.

KOF.sentence: The Latin American Coca-Cola bottler is quiet, sitting at the cheaper end of its usual range while paying a decent dividend — a boring, steady one.

PBR.A.sentence: Petrobras is flat, and while its earnings look cheap on the surface, that's mostly because oil prices are high right now — which is more a warning that profits could roll over than a sign of a bargain.

AMKBY.sentence: Maersk, the Danish container-shipping giant, is calm this week, and its stock is priced below the value of its own ships — roughly what it'd cost to rebuild the fleet.

MA.sentence: Mastercard was flat, and it's trading unusually close to Visa despite no real problem in the business — people keep tapping their cards more than ever.

ISRG.sentence: No fresh data on the da Vinci surgical robot company today, so there's nothing new to report.
